entries.content: Maddison (2003) historical GDP per capita data. Decennial observations (10-year intervals) from 1710 to 2000. 206 columns covering world regions and individual countries. Key derived series: RICHEST 4 and POOREST 4 country averages. Kuwait, Qatar, and Venezuela are excluded from the top four when their inclusion would dramatically overstate the average (oil-rich distortion). Asia includes Japan, South Korea, oil-rich countries; Africa includes South Africa and Egypt - understates true rich-poor divergence.

Maddison, A. (2003). The World Economy: Historical Statistics. Paris: OECD Development Centre. GDP per capita in 1990 international Geary-Khamis dollars. Decennial data for world regions and 200+ countries. Maddison Project Database extends and revises these estimates.

Figs 2.15-2.17 display global income divergence. Fig 2.15: world and regional GDP per capita. Fig 2.16: ratio of richest to poorest regions. Fig 2.17: RICHEST 4 vs POOREST 4 countries (with Kuwait, Qatar, Venezuela excluded when distorting). Wide-table format - 206 columns, no single final series. Last column 'formula check' can be ignored. Extension pending - requires Maddison Project Database 2023 parsing.

citations.full: Maddison, A. (2003). The World Economy: Historical Statistics. Paris: OECD Development Centre. https://www.rug.nl/ggdc/historicaldevelopment/maddison/
Maddison Project Database. Bolt, J. and van Zanden, J.L. (2020). Maddison Style Estimates of the Evolution of the World Economy. Journal of Economic Surveys.

methodology_summary: S017 uses Maddison (2003) GDP per capita in 1990 international Geary-Khamis dollars. Decennial data (1710-2000) for world, regions (Western Europe, Western Offshoots, Latin America, Asia, Africa), and 200+ countries. RICHEST 4 and POOREST 4 are calculated averages; Kuwait, Qatar, Venezuela excluded from top four when inclusion would overstate the average. Classified as wide_table format. No extension yet - Maddison Project Database 2023 parsing required.
known_issues: GDP per capita for poorest countries may be misleading - 'distribution of income in poorer countries is appallingly skewed' (appendix_methodology_summary)
Asia includes Japan, South Korea, oil-rich countries; Africa includes South Africa, Egypt - understates true divergence
No extension - requires Maddison Project Database 2023 parsing (separate task)
Validator V9: No final series column - wide_table format, all IDs have letter suffixes

cross_references.relationship: US GDP context - S003 is US GDP, S017 is global comparison
